evals.expected_output: A single well-formed conventional commit message with type 'fix', scope 'loader-service', an imperative subject line under 72 chars, and a body explaining that calling cancel() on a null future caused an NPE when a loader had never been scheduled before being rescheduled.
expectations: The subject line is under 72 characters
The subject line uses imperative mood (e.g., 'guard', 'add null check', 'prevent')
The body or subject explains WHY the null check is needed (e.g., loader not yet scheduled, first reschedule)
Only one commit message is proposed (this is a single-concern change)

evals.expected_output: Two separate conventional commit messages: one for the loader deep-link feature (feat, masros-gui scope), and one for the new Inputs panel in the flow step editor (feat, masros-gui scope). Each has a clear subject and a body paragraph explaining the motivation.
expectations: At least two commit messages are proposed (the loader deep-link and the Inputs panel are distinct features)
Each subject line is under 72 characters
The loader commit explains that it enables deep-linking to a loader by YML code via URL param
The Inputs panel commit explains what the new panel shows (routing, parent flows, loaders, mass jobs)
At least one commit message has a body paragraph (not just a subject line)

evals.expected_output: One or two conventional commit messages. The key choices: the producer tuning (linger, batch, compression, buffer) is a 'perf' type change. The KafkaStepLogPublisher extraction is a 'refactor'. A good answer might combine them into one message or split them, but must use the right types and explain the motivation (reducing duplicate Kafka serialization code / improving throughput via batching).
expectations: At least one commit uses type 'refactor' or 'perf' (not 'feat' or 'fix' — nothing new is being added and there's no bug being fixed from the user's perspective)
The subject line(s) are under 72 characters each
The message(s) explain the motivation — either reducing duplicated serialization code, or improving throughput via batching/compression
No message uses type 'feat' (this is a refactoring/performance change, not a new user-facing feature)
